title: elymus lanceolatus subsp. albicans
intro: Elymus lanceolatus subsp. albicans, also known as the white wildrye or white hair grass, is a subspecies of a perennial grass native to North America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Elymus lanceolatus subsp. albicans include soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems such as bronchitis. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by steeping the dried leaves and stems in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
